episode_1: Initial run through with all positive states. No assistant.
episode_2: Too small of a first gather
episode_3: Too deep of a first gather
episode_6: Air marvering with the glass too onto the pipe
episode_7: Showing the effect of blowing a bubble with the marvering on the pipe
episode_11: The first part of blocking too cold
episode_14: Blocking into an irrecoverable shape, folding in on itself
episode_15: Too small of a first gather (2)
episode_16: Too small of a second gather (2)
episode_18: Too deep of a second gather (2)
episode_19: Air marvering too much onto the pipe (2)

episode_23: Too deep of a first gather (2)
episode_26: Positive-neutral run through partial #2. Some issues: assisting started halfway through, blow issue, punty wasn't perfect, centering step required. Died after first sofi.
episode_27: Too hot second gather without sufficient turning while closing door
episode_28: Neutral run through. Issues include too long between dips, releasing cap too soon, capping after bubble has blown out
episode_29: Too hot second gather without sufficient turning while closing door
episode_30: Neutral run-through. Issues: Capped off center, which made walls blow out and stretch unevenly
episode_38: Decent pint glass emerged. Multiple issue that were corrected with frequent reheating.
episode_39: Wonderful nonic glass but demonstrated bad punty break off at the end. Seedy glass all throughout
episode_40: Deliberately placed punty off center and had lip opening issues
episode_41: Jack line off center leading to an amorphous lip
episode_42: Greg making votive, bottom eventually was too messed up
episode_43: The furnace was acting up so there was a bit of a traffic jam during gathering. Using a blowhose, inflated while adding the jackline and stretched over the side of the bench in tandem. Multiple rounds of sretching, blowing, and reheating. Self-described issues were the lip getting too thick causing it to go off center. There was a a thinning procedure, but the thick lip made the bonk-off uneven. Used the marver rather than the block.
episode_44: Only a single gather this time, and using the blocks rather than the marver. During sphere inflation, used the back of the jacks to chill the bottom half. Question: there inflation going on during the hanging steps? The bottom cracked during the lip opening due to the bottom being too thin: blew it out too much, there's a fine line and you're risking it if the bottom is thin. Multiple chilling passes with open jack blades after pretty much every step on the punty. The cup came off the pipe but had a hole.
episode_45: This take will involve deliberate mistakes. The first mistake will be blowing the bubble off center. Next is a completely vertical jack line that is missing a shoulder. Then blowing it too hard on the paddle to introduce an unintentional waist. Next mistake is getting the piece too hot when the punty is attached, causing a sag which makes the entire piece off center and risks fusing the punty. Then, the hole is not opened up enough before inflated with the sofietta, causing it to go off center. Also, before using the sofietta, you must chill the sides and walls, otherwise the entire cup will balloon. If you attempt to oppen the hole when it's too small, too cold, or use too much force, it will open eccentrically. Then, incorrect angle of the jacks caused a flare out. Over all the reheating, heated the punty too far in the hole caused the bottom to sag inwards which caused the bottom to fuse and break off badly.
episode_46: Accidental time lapse!! Generally working too cold on this one. Started with a cold-ish pipe. Too cold cutting in the jack line.
episode_47: [vertical] Continuing the too cold example with normal video. Touched one side more than the other so that it will inflate more on the other side. Paddled when too cold = dome of doom, people try compensate by pushing harder. Then, the jack line was too wide causing it to break off in a chipped way. The punty was on the colder side but still stuck well.  The lip is opened way too cold, causing lopsided edges all throughout.
